claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with a basic question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have an 18-year-old son named Alex who lives with you.

Since Alex is 18 (over 16), a key factor for Child Benefit eligibility is whether he's in approved education or training. Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they remain in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he in sixth form, college, doing A-levels, or an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and he's just been looking for work, really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex is 18 and not currently in approved education or training, this is an important factor.

Let me ask a follow-up question:

**Has Alex started any paid work? And if so, is he working 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't started any job yet. He's still looking for something, but he's not working at all right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. One more important question:

**Is Alex currently claiming any benefits in his own right, such as Universal Credit, Employment and Support Allowance, or any other benefits?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not claiming any benefits himself at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit based on what you've told me.

Based on the rules, I need to inform you that unfortunately, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex in these circumstances. Here's why:

**For children aged 16-19**, Child Benefit can continue, but only if they are in **approved education or training**. Since Alex is 18 and is not currently in any approved education or training, he doesn't meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit, even though he lives with you and is not working.

The fact that he's looking for work and not claiming benefits himself doesn't change this - the key requirement is that he must be in approved education or training to qualify for Child Benefit beyond age 16.

Let me submit my formal assessment now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to confirm that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex at this time. 

However, if Alex's circumstances change - for example, if he enrolls in college, sixth form, or an approved training course - you may be able to claim Child Benefit again, as long as he remains under 20 years old and stays in that approved education or training.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and lives with the claimant. While the claimant is responsible for Alex and he lives with them, Alex is over 16 years old. According to the rules, Child Benefit can continue for children aged 16-19 only if they stay in approved education or training. Alex is not currently in any approved education or training - he has finished school and is looking for work. Although he is not working 24 hours or more per week and is not claiming any benefits in his own right, the absence of approved education or training means the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one son, Alex, who is 18 years old and lives with them. However, Alex is not in approved education or training, which is a mandatory requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16-19. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

However, Alex is not in approved education or training, which is a mandatory requirement for Child Benefit eligibility for children aged 16-19. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
